content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 6, 1994

Beth Reno, Treasurer
Steve Peace for Senate
P. O. Box 1515
Chula Vista, CA 91912

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-94-123

Dear Ms. Reno:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act "the Act".1/ Because your
questions are general in nature, we consider your letter to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
18329(c)(3).2/

QUESTIONS

1. May petty cash funds be used to make expenditures for
individual items that sell for under $100?

2. Must a candidate file a campaign statement for an active
controlled committee when the candidate files campaign statements
in connection with terminating other controlled committees?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Petty cash funds may be used to make campaign related
expenditures under $100.

2. A candidate is not required to file a campaign statement
for an active controlled committee when the candidate files
campaign statements in connection with terminating other
controlled committees.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Beth Reno
Our File No. I-94-123
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Proposition 73, passed by the voters in June of 1988, added
provisions to the Act requiring all campaign related expenditures
made by a candidate to be made from a campaign bank account.
(Section 85201.) However, Regulation 18524(d) (copy enclosed)
provides that a candidate may establish, for the campaign bank
account, one petty cash fund at each campaign office. No more
than $100 may be deposited in the petty cash fund at any time, and
no expenditure totaling $100 or more may be made from the petty
cash fund.

Petty cash funds may be used to purchase multiple items from
a single vendor if the total amount of the expenditure does not
total $100 or more during any individual transaction. For
example, petty cash funds totaling under $100 may be used to
purchase miscellaneous supplies at an office supply store. The
next day or later in the same day, petty cash funds totaling under
$100 may also be used to purchase additional supplies from the
same store.

The Act requires candidates and committees to file campaign
statements disclosing contributions received and expenditures
made. The filing deadlines and periods covered by the campaign
statements are specified in Sections 84200-84204.

Section 84214 requires committees which receive contributions
and candidates to terminate their filing obligations pursuant to
regulations adopted by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
Pursuant to Regulation 18404(b)(4), a committee may terminate when
the committee has filed all required campaign statements
disclosing all reportable transactions. There is no requirement
under the Act for a candidate to file a campaign statement for an
active controlled committee when the candidate files a campaign
statement for the sole purpose of terminating another controlled
committee.

If you have any questions or need further information, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
